name: Chocolate Walnut Cookies
recipeIngredient: 16 ounces semisweet chocolate, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. In the bowl of an electric mixer, beat the eggs and sugar for 20 minutes or until the mixture is thick and pale.
2. Add the melted chocolate, butter, vanilla, espresso, flour, salt, and baking powder to the egg mixture. Beat on low until combined well.
3. Gradually add the chocolate chips and walnuts, beating until the dough is smooth.
4. Form the dough into a log about 4 inches in diameter, wrap it in wax paper, and chill for 3 hours or until firm.
5. Cut the log into 1-inch-thick rounds and arrange them 2 inches apart on baking sheets lined with parchment paper.
6. Bake in batches in a preheated oven at 350°F for 15 minutes, or until the tops are shiny and cracked slightly (the cookies will be very soft).
7. Transfer the baking sheets to racks and let the cookies cool completely before removing them from the sheets (they will firm up as they cool).
Makes about 12 cookies.
